title: Validate native iOS/Android STT for meditation use
description:
Pilot `SFSpeechRecognizer` (iOS) and Android `SpeechRecognizer` via a Capacitor plugin to confirm they handle meditation-specific speech patterns before committing to the no-Whisper-on-mobile strategy.

Test cases that may break native APIs (which are tuned for Siri-style queries):
- **Long pauses** without the recognizer giving up / cutting off
- **Soft, whispered, or quiet speech** (relevant during deep meditation)
- **Brief 1–2 word noting utterances** ('warmth', 'tightness', 'sound')
- **Half-finished sentences** trailing into pauses
- **iOS aggressive silence cutoff** — defaults to ~1.5s. Configurable?

Deliverable: a quick prototype Capacitor app or test page that runs the speech recognizer with realistic meditation input. Decision output: do we go native, or do we need a cloud-Whisper fallback path for mobile?

If native fails: streaming audio to a hosted Whisper endpoint is still cheaper than bundling the model. Document the fallback architecture if needed.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 mobile architecture conversation.

title: Validate on-device LLM quality for facilitator role
description:
Pilot small on-device LLMs to confirm they can hold up as the default mode for the standalone mobile app. The 'on-device by default, your meditations never leave your phone' pitch only works if the on-device experience is genuinely good — not a crippled trial pushing users toward the paid cloud tier.

Candidates to test:
- **Apple Intelligence Foundation Models** (iOS 18+ on A17 Pro / M-series). Free, on-device, no bundling. Most attractive if quality holds.
- **Gemma 2/3 (2B–9B)** via MLC-LLM or llama.cpp (mobile builds exist)
- **Phi-3 / Phi-4 mini**
- **Llama 3.2 (1B / 3B)**

Quality bar: must handle the practices that distinguish aloud. — not just generic supportive presence.
- Body / emotions / parts work prompts
- Directiveness slider responsiveness (especially mid-to-high)
- Recognition of subtle states (jhana factors, somatic shifts)
- The `[HOLD]` signal behavior (silence mode entry)
- Coherent multi-turn over a 20–40 minute session
- Doesn't drift into ChatGPT-as-monk vibes

Deliverable: model recommendation, a 'this is the on-device experience' demo session, and an honest writeup of where it falls short relative to Sonnet — those gaps inform the cloud-upgrade pitch.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 mobile architecture conversation. Blocks dbd's design (default tier viability).

title: Phase 3A: Modularize session.js
Live credit-use meter / cost transparency in session UI
description:
Show users their compute spend in real time / per session — e.g. a small live meter during a session and a 'this session used ~2.3 cents of compute' summary after. Radical-transparency UX that reinforces the project's values guardrail (meditation-pal-a2j: 'you're buying compute, not calm'): most apps hide unit cost; surfacing it builds trust instead of spending it, and it fits the aloud. brand.

Scope:
- Live credit/cost readout during a session (debits as LLM tokens + STT minutes + TTS chars accrue).
- Post-session summary of spend.
- Works for both metered credit users and (informationally) BYOK/free users.
- Honest rounding; don't make the meter anxiety-inducing — calm, ambient, optional to expand.

Depends on the metered billing engine (meditation-pal-8sj) for the underlying per-request cost data. Relates to meditation-pal-a2j (values/copy). Child of the web demo epic but applies to mobile too.

title: Server-side TTS for web (Piper or ElevenLabs) behind tts-picker
description: Decide and wire the web demo's voice. browser-tts.ts (speechSynthesis) is free/client-side but voice quality varies by device/OS ('crap voice' risk). For 'non-crap' consistent voice, add a server TTS path: Piper (free, CPU on our box) as default, ElevenLabs (per-char, metered) as premium/credit-spend option. Slot behind ts/ui/src/adapters/tts-picker.ts alongside server-tts.ts. ElevenLabs cost feeds the metered billing engine. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.
notes: TTS PROVIDER LANDSCAPE (web-verified 2026-05, all per-1M-chars). Cheapest-good tier ~$7-16/1M: Hume Octave ~$7.60 (emotion-native, BEST FIT for calm meditation delivery + cheap), Unreal Speech ~$8 (cheapest commercial, steep volume tiers), Speechify ~$10, OpenAI tts-1 $15, Deepgram Aura-1 $15 (low latency), Azure Neural ~$14, Google Neural2/Polly ~$16, Fish Audio s2-pro $15. Mid ~$30-50: Deepgram Aura-2 $30, Cartesia Sonic $35-50 (sub-100ms latency), ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 $50 (cut from $110 in 2026). Premium ~$100: ElevenLabs Multilingual v2 ~$100. Open/self-host floor: Kokoro 82B ~$0.70/1M (but self-hosting breaks the cheap stateless-proxy model, same tradeoff as self-hosting Whisper). RECOMMENDATION: default cloud voice to Hume Octave or OpenAI/neural (~half-or-less of ElevenLabs Flash, plenty for calm guidance); offer ElevenLabs as premium upsell; keep free local (MS Edge=Azure neural, Apple Premium) as default when detected. Table in ts/server/src/pricing/voices.ts has hume/openai/neural/deepgram/elevenlabs entries; others noted in code comments. STT rate CORRECTED: Groq whisper-large-v3-turbo is $0.04/hr (not $0.111 — that's large-v3).
close_reason: Implemented with Google Cloud TTS (chosen over ElevenLabs/Piper for cost/quality): POST /v1/tts (providers/tts.ts synthesizeWithGoogle, routes/tts.ts), metered per-char (priceTtsChars), audio/mpeg + cost headers. UI: ServerTtsEngine gained usePost+authProvider; tts-picker.createServerAloudTts; session.ts routes TTS through server on hosted provider. Needs GOOGLE_TTS_API_KEY.

title: Free-tier gating + abuse/quota controls
description: Give each verified account 1-2 free sessions on the CHEAPEST model (so burner-account abuse is cheap to absorb). Layer IP/device heuristics and rate-limits for determined abusers; accept some leakage. This is load-bearing for the web demo specifically because — unlike mobile (gated by app-store distribution) — the web is open to the internet, so free sessions are an unguarded cost faucet. Lives in/with the standalone proxy. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.
notes: FREE GRANT DECISION (with dev): 20 credits per new VERIFIED (Google/Apple, unique email) account. At USD_PER_CREDIT $0.05 (Model B, credits debit at cost) that's a hard $1.00 of provider cost per signup — the grant size IS the per-account abuse blast radius. Buys ~10 value-tier sessions (Gemini+local voice), ~3 mid (Sonnet+Hume), or ~1 premium (Opus+ElevenLabs) — enough for a real trial + conversion pressure while the habit is fresh. Default set in config.ts (ALOUD_FREE_SIGNUP_CREDITS=20). Backstops: steer free tier to the value-default model/voice so the grant stretches; RateGuard + verified-email gate for velocity abuse. Floor is safe — once history caching (meditation-pal-cet) lands, 20 stretches further, not less.

title: Capacitor native wrapper for app stores
description:
Wrap the web app with Capacitor for iOS App Store and Google Play submission.

Architecture decisions (2026-05-09):
- **STT**: use native iOS `SFSpeechRecognizer` and Android `SpeechRecognizer` via a Capacitor plugin (e.g. `@capacitor-community/speech-recognition`). Do NOT bundle Whisper on mobile — strips ~150MB+ from the app and avoids battery/memory pain. Quality validation needed first (see meditation-specific risks ticket).
- **TTS**: use native `AVSpeechSynthesizer` (iOS) / `TextToSpeech` (Android) by default; ElevenLabs as premium upgrade. Drop Piper bundling on mobile.
- **LLM**: tiered (on-device small model / BYOK / hosted cloud) — see meditation-pal-dbd.
- **Backend**: open question whether to keep Python (server runs separately, mobile talks to hosted endpoint) or rewrite in TypeScript so it can run inside Capacitor as JS — see backend rewrite decision ticket.
- **Privacy disclosure**: be transparent if native STT falls back to Apple/Google cloud; meditators care a lot about their words not leaving the device.

Needs responsive layout (done), PWA, mobile audio quirks done first.
notes: 2026-05-27 shell-decision review: the 'drop Whisper/Piper on mobile, use native SFSpeechRecognizer + AVSpeechSynthesizer' decision (2026-05-09) is REOPENED for reconsideration. Findings: (1) Piper runs offline + low-latency on both platforms as of 2026 and reportedly beats Apple Premium voices — prefer bundling Piper over native TTS. (2) On-device whisper.cpp is faster-than-realtime on iPhone 13+ (Core ML/ANE) and, because aloud is turn-based not live-dictation, can afford small.en for quality; it also sidesteps native STT's Siri-tuned pause/soft-speech cutoffs (the meditation-pal-0ao risk). (3) Free/private on-device is the user's stated preference. Net: keep on-device Whisper+Piper alive as the default tier on capable devices; native STT/TTS demoted to fallback. This is Tauri's main mobile advantage (in-process Rust ML) — see the full brief on meditation-pal-nn1, which now blocks this ticket. Decide on-device-vs-native AFTER the nn1 shell spike.

title: Remove CLI mode (src/__main__.py, src/main.py)
Post-parity: decouple TS UI from the wholesale Flask stylesheet @import
description:
The TS UI gets its visual identity by @import-ing the entire Flask stylesheet (src/web/static/css/style.css, ~91KB) into ts/ui/src/style.css, and by reusing Flask's class names verbatim (.participant-*, .session-item, .voice-modal-*, .stepper, etc.). This was the RIGHT call during the migration — it's why parity has been fast and cheap — but once the TS app is the source of truth (esp. if/when the Flask desktop app is deprecated), revisit it.

Downsides to clean up at that point:
- Dead weight: the TS bundle ships the whole Flask sheet incl. rules for things the TS app doesn't use or does differently (pywebview/desktop-only chrome, update banner, tour, etc.). ~12KB gzipped, not huge, but not minimal.
- Coupling both ways: editing Flask CSS silently changes the TS app (we hit this with the orb z-index, breathe keyframes, about-modal). Good while shared; a liability once they diverge.
- Class-name coupling: TS markup depends on Flask's exact class names; a Flask refactor would break TS.
- Build coupling: vite imports CSS from outside ui/ (../../../src/web/static), tying the build to the Flask tree layout.
- No design tokens of our own: TS-only layout used magic numbers (mostly removed now by reusing Flask classes); a tokenized system would be cleaner long-term.

Proposed when at parity: copy the actually-used CSS into ts/ui (or extract a shared package), tree-shake/split it, stop importing the whole sheet, and consider tokenizing. Decide based on whether Flask stays (keep shared) or is retired (TS owns CSS). Relates to meditation-pal-1au (CSS-magic-numbers note) and the parity-audit ticket.
notes: REFRAME (2026-05-26): Python/Flask is being removed entirely — the whole app becomes one TS codebase (desktop via Tauri/Capacitor, web, iOS, Android). So this isn't optional cleanup: when src/web/ is deleted, the ts/ui/src/style.css '@import url(../../../src/web/static/css/style.css)' breaks. Relocating that stylesheet into ts/ (TS owns its CSS) becomes a REQUIRED step of the Python-removal work, not a nice-to-have. The two-way-coupling concern is moot (no Flask to share with); the 'TS must own + can trim dead rules' concern is now the whole point.

title: Dev cutover: point Vite /app proxy at Hono (not Flask) so browser preview is Flask-free
description:
PROBLEM (observed on ts-core, 2026-05-29): hitting the dev server at :1420 (`tauri dev` runs Vite via beforeDevCommand) in a plain browser, every /app/v1/* call dies because the Vite proxy rewrites /app/v1 -> /api and targets Flask (:4649), which tauri:dev does NOT start. Symptom: Ollama (and providers/models/voices generally) appear 'not installed'/empty. Flask is being removed entirely, so the dev/browser-preview backend must stop depending on it.

TARGET: in ui/vite.config.ts, point the '/app' proxy at the Hono server (SERVER_URL, :8787) and drop the '/api' rewrite — Hono serves /app/v1/* natively (routes/app.ts). Then the browser preview needs the Hono server running (`ts/server`), not Flask. Desktop keeps using the Tauri Rust backend.

ENDPOINT GAP AUDIT (UI appUrl() calls vs Hono routes/app.ts today):
  Hono serves: /system-info, /providers, /models/:provider, /voices (stub -> []).
  MISSING on Hono, needed for browser preview:
    - /voices/preview  (voice preview audio) — not implemented
    - /voices          (real catalog; currently returns []) — decide static catalog for web
  Desktop-only (correctly NOT on Hono; Rust backend serves them; must stay gated off in a plain browser via is-desktop/capabilities so they don't 404-spam):
    - /llm/anthropic/messages, /llm/claude_proxy/complete
    - /ollama/{pull,delete,restart,upgrade}, /install/ollama
    - /open-config-folder, /open-sessions-folder, /open-voice-settings

ALREADY HANDLED (no regression from cutover): Ollama detection is now Flask-independent — capabilities.ts probes /ollama directly and model-picker.ts + settings-ollama.ts fall back to ollama-direct.ts when the app backend reports nothing (commit bc1460e). Hono hardcodes ollama.available=false; the direct probe covers the browser-dev case.

ACCEPTANCE: with Flask NOT running and the Hono server up, load :1420 in a browser — providers/models/voices populate, voice preview works, Ollama detected via direct probe, and no desktop-only endpoint is called (no 404 spam). Update the vite.config.ts comment. 

Relates to bkg (Hono endpoints) and is a concrete prerequisite for sk8 (which lists 'the Vite proxy Flask target' among deletion targets).

title: TS parity: per-session usage tracking (LLM tokens split + STT secs + TTS chars) — match Python session-JSON schema
description:
Mirror the Python per-session usage tracking (implemented 2026-05-24) in the TypeScript rewrite, once the TS orchestration core (meditation-pal-2qg) is functional. DO NOT build now — TS isn't runnable, so the code couldn't be exercised and would drift.

The contract is the persisted session-JSON schema (so saved sessions stay cross-compatible between the Python desktop app and the TS web/mobile app). Match exactly what Python now writes:

Per ASSISTANT exchange produced by a completion (omit on user turns and static/fallback messages):
  tokens_in, tokens_out  (ints; required together when present)
  cache_read, cache_creation  (ints; include only when non-zero)

Per-session "usage" block (always present):
  llm_calls            int  — every completion, incl. off-transcript (summary/resume-intent/noting labels)
  llm_tokens_in        int
  llm_tokens_out       int
  llm_cache_read       int
  llm_cache_creation   int
  stt_seconds          float (rounded 2dp) — ALL transcriptions incl. speculative/command (real STT compute)
  tts_chars            int  — server-side synthesis only; browser speechSynthesis not counted

Keep input and output SEPARATE, never summed: output costs ~4-5x input on Claude, and cache reads ~10x less than fresh input — the split is load-bearing for the cost meter and margin math.

Reference implementation (Python, copy the semantics):
- src/llm/base.py CompletionResult: input_tokens/output_tokens/cache_read_tokens/cache_creation_tokens (TS already has CompletionResult.tokensUsed in ts/src/llm/base.ts but only the SUM — needs the split; the TS providers already parse usage from responses, they just discard the breakdown).
- src/facilitation/session.py: Exchange token fields + SessionUsage dataclass + SessionManager.add_assistant_message(tokens...) / record_llm_usage / record_stt / record_tts; usage folded in to_dict. Mirror in ts/src/facilitation/session.ts (Exchange + SessionState + SessionStore serialization).
- Capture points: LLM at the generate-response equivalent (+ off-transcript calls); STT at the transcription handler; TTS at the server-synthesis path.

This is the data substrate under meditation-pal-14s (live cost meter) and meditation-pal-8sj (metered billing). NOTE: Python web also wired STT seconds + server-TTS chars; the Python CLI path captures LLM tokens via the shared session layer but does not yet tally STT/TTS — minor follow-up if desired.
notes: CLI note correction (2026-05-24): there is no CLI path — src/main.py was removed and src/__main__.py just delegates to src.web. So the Python web app is the only entry point; the full triad (LLM tokens + STT secs + TTS chars) is captured there. Ignore the earlier 'CLI doesn't tally STT/TTS' aside.
close_reason: Implemented on ts-core. CompletionResult + StreamChunk now carry the input/output/cacheRead/cacheCreation split (kept separate, never summed; tokensUsed retained as back-compat sum). All 4 providers parse it: anthropic (incl. message_start+message_delta merge for cache tokens), openai (prompt/completion), ollama (prompt_eval/eval, no cache), claude-proxy (incl. cache). Exchange gained tokensIn/tokensOut/cacheRead/cacheCreation (cache omitted when 0, matching Python); SessionState gained a SessionUsage tally; SessionManager.addAssistantMessage(content,name,usage) folds usage in + recordLlmUsage/recordStt/recordTts mirror Python semantics. Capture points wired in the web UI: streaming response + continuation opener (on-transcript), summary (off-transcript, folded before save), server-Whisper STT seconds (on-device engines omit), server-TTS chars (browser TTS not counted). Off-transcript helpers (generateSessionSummary, generateNotingLabel) gained an onUsage callback. 17 new tests (134 total pass); typecheck + ui build clean. Wire-format (camelCase vs Python snake_case) parity tracked separately — see new P3 follow-up.

title: In-session mobile reachability: floating hamburger + session-only More items
description: The bottom-nav is hidden during a session (body[data-session-active] .bottom-nav{display:none} in the imported CSS), so on a phone mid-session there's still no way to reach End/History. Old app injected a floating hamburger (data-mobile-more-open) on the session page that opened the More sheet's session-only items. title: OpenRouter/OpenAI provider: parse cached_tokens so Gemini/DeepSeek caching is billed correctly
notes: The OpenAI-compatible provider (used for OpenRouter) doesn't parse prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens, so Gemini/DeepSeek implicit caching isn't captured in usage. Effect: cache reads bill at full input rate -> we OVER-bill value models (safe, but not ideal). pricing/providers.ts currently models gemini-2.5-flash-lite cacheRead at full input rate to match this. Fix: read cached_tokens into CompletionResult.cacheReadTokens; then add the real cache rate (Gemini ~75-90% off, DeepSeek ~98% off) and these models get even cheaper. Blocks accurate value-tier pricing on meditation-pal-7xl.

title: Availability-driven source menus (LLM + TTS): show only what the environment can reach
description:
Broad rule (from dev, 2026-05-26): every menu should show only sources actually usable in the current environment. Voices already do this (hosted voices appear only when GET /v1/voices is non-empty; commit b2be95a). Extend the same to the LLM-source and TTS-source menus:

- LLM source list (ALL_PROVIDERS in ui/src/settings.ts) currently shows every provider always, gated only by desktopOnly (claude_proxy) and needsKey. Should reflect reachability: 'aloud (hosted)' only when the server /health is reachable; ollama only when a local Ollama responds; claude_proxy only when the local CLI bridge exists; BYOK providers always (user can add a key). On the website build, local options shouldn't appear.
- TTS source: same idea — browser speechSynthesis always; Flask server voices only when Flask reachable; hosted Google only when server reachable (voices already handle this).
- Probe signals already exist: server /health (providers/stt/tts), Flask /api/providers + /api/voices, browser capability checks. Needs a small capability-detection layer the menus consult, with caching + re-probe on session start (mirrors invalidateSttBackendCache).

Part of the web-demo epic (meditation-pal-bot). The aloud-website experience especially depends on this (no local runtimes).
notes:
Concrete gaps found while tracing the voice logic (2026-05-26):

WHAT'S ALREADY RIGHT:
- Piper/macOS voices: sourced only from Flask /api/voices via fetchServerVoices(); unreachable in server mode → returns null → they don't show. Correct, but IMPLICIT (availability-by-fetch-failure, not an explicit guard).
- Hosted Google voices: gated on GET /v1/voices being non-empty (done).
- claude_proxy: desktopOnly, hidden when Flask /api/system-info unreachable.

GAPS TO FIX (the inverse problems):
1. Ollama is in ALL_PROVIDERS WITHOUT desktopOnly → 'Ollama (Local)' shows on the website where no local Ollama exists. Needs a local/availability gate (probe, or a localOnly flag like desktopOnly).
2. 'aloud (hosted)' shows unconditionally → appears even in a pure local app with no server running (would fail). Gate on server /health reachability.
3. Settings page Flask/desktop-only surfaces not fully gated for server mode: Piper download/uninstall (/api/tts/*), 'Download Premium voices', 'Open config folder', env-var hints, Ollama install/upgrade tools. Hide these when not local/Flask.

FRAMING: model THREE environment axes, not one binary (current detectIsDesktop = 'Flask present = desktop'):
  - local/Flask present → Piper/macOS voices, claude_proxy, Ollama, config-folder, Piper mgmt
  - hosted server reachable → 'aloud (hosted)', hosted voices (done), hosted STT
  - browser capabilities → Web Speech voices
A small capability layer the menus + settings sections consult (cached, re-probe on session start, mirroring invalidateSttBackendCache). Probe signals already exist: Flask /api/system-info + /api/providers + /api/voices; server /health (providers/stt/tts); browser checks.
close_reason: Availability-driven menus implemented. capabilities.ts probes three axes (flask /api/system-info, hosted /v1/me/models, ollama /ollama/api/tags), cached at boot. LLM provider menu now gates per ProviderMeta.requires via isProviderAvailable: aloud→hosted, ollama→ollama, claude_proxy→flask, BYOK always (setup.ts + settings.ts). Voices already availability-driven (Flask voices via fetch-null; hosted via /v1/voices). Settings' Flask-only surfaces gated (Piper mgmt renders only on server voices; config-folder via isDesktopSync). core 151, server 68 green. NOTE: the residual 'Flask still serves Piper/voices/STT' is PORT-COMPLETION debt (move those to TS-native runtimes), not menu logic — tracked under the TS migration, not here.

title: Phase 4A: Pytest Tests
TS↔Python session-JSON wire-format parity (camelCase vs snake_case)
description:
The TS app persists SessionState directly to localStorage in camelCase (sessionId, startTime, exchanges[].tokensIn, usage.llmTokensIn, ...). The Python desktop app writes sessions/*.json in snake_case with extra derived fields (session_id, start_time, duration, exchange_count, exchanges[].time ISO string, usage.{llm_tokens_in,...,stt_seconds rounded 2dp, tts_chars}). 

Usage-tracking SEMANTICS now match across both (done in 6j5: input/output kept separate, cache fields, llm_calls counts off-transcript, stt_seconds for server-Whisper only, tts_chars for server synthesis only). What does NOT match is the on-the-wire field naming/shape — the two apps use separate stores today, so this only bites if/when sessions are exchanged between them (hosted web import/export, mobile↔desktop sync). 

When that need is real: add a serialization boundary (toPythonSessionJson / fromPythonSessionJson) rather than renaming the TS internal fields, so the UI (history.ts etc., all camelCase readers) stays untouched. Reference: src/facilitation/session.py to_dict() shapes.

title: LLM efficiency: prompt prefix caching for hosted API (quality-neutral cost win)
description:
QUALITY-NEUTRAL cost optimization for the hosted API path. Prefix caching changes how the SAME input is billed/stored on Anthropic's side — it does NOT trim, summarize, or alter any tokens. The model sees identical context and produces identical output; only cost (and slightly, latency) change. The only failure mode is a cache MISS = pay full price that turn = today's behavior. No quality-degradation path exists. (Contrast: context-size reduction DOES change what the model sees — split out to a separate low-priority ticket.)

CONTEXT (measured 2026-05-24): config uses FULL context. A ~35-min, ~33-turn session re-sends the whole transcript every turn: ~76k tokens of history input + ~51k system-prompt input (~1.56k x 33) vs ~2.7k output. Input is ~98% of volume. The system prompt is ALREADY cached (cache_control in src/llm/anthropic.py); the ~76k history is not.

WHO THIS HELPS: hosted API / future paying users only. The dev's own sessions are on the Claude subscription (flat, not per-token — caching saves nothing) or local Gemma (no token bill). So this is a SERVICE-economics lever, not a local-session change.

THE CHANGE (small, bounded): in src/llm/anthropic.py, add a cache breakpoint (cache_control: ephemeral) on the LAST message of the conversation history each turn, so Anthropic caches the full prefix (system + all prior turns). Next turn the prior prefix is a cache-READ at ~10% input price.
Handle: up to 4 breakpoints allowed; 5-min cache TTL (fine — turns are seconds apart); min cacheable prefix ~1024 tokens Sonnet/Opus (~2048 Haiku), met after a few turns; cache WRITE costs 1.25x on the turn created. Keep request prefix byte-stable (history is append-only, so this holds naturally).
Estimated impact: ~76k full-price history input -> mostly cache-reads => roughly 2-3x lower cost on long sessions (hypothetical Opus session ~$1.40 -> ~$0.45). Makes Opus-for-exploration viability much better.

SCOPE: Anthropic API provider first (+ a test asserting the breakpoint is set and a usage-based check). OpenAI/OpenRouter have their own auto-caching (verify, likely no code). Ollama/local + claude_proxy(subscription): N/A.
MINOR (opportunistic): trim redundant retained content (e.g. [HOLD] prefixes) if cheap.

Relates: meditation-pal-vd3 (proxy — where this lives for the service), meditation-pal-8sj (billing math benefits), meditation-pal-14s (cost-meter numbers drop). Latency/context companion: see the split-out local-context ticket.

title: Comms strategy: communicate device capability via runtime detection + honest practice-tiering (no spec tables)
description:
STRATEGY / direction note — draft actual copy LATER (text will change; capture the approach now).

Communicate on-device capability via RUNTIME DETECTION + honest practice-tiering, NEVER device spec tables. Solves cross-platform fragmentation (esp. Android's many manufacturers) by never enumerating devices: read available RAM at first launch, pick the model tier live, and tell the user what THEIR phone can do.

Why: free app => no refund/chargeback exposure on the download, but App Store/Play REVIEWS are the real risk. A static "runs great on your phone" claim is false on 4GB devices (iPhone 13 floor) and earns 1-star "the AI is dumb on my phone" reviews. Honest, detected expectation-setting avoids that.

Three layers, each says a different amount:
1. STORE LISTING — say the least, lead with values; no device-performance claims (can't be conditional, they rot). One honest sentence, e.g.: "aloud runs a private AI on your device, free — nothing leaves your phone. Newer phones run a larger model; on any phone you can connect your own AI key or add hosted sessions for the most in-depth practices." Borrow Apple's "Optimized for newer devices" register. On Play Console: set a real RAM/API-level floor in the device catalog so Play auto-filters phones too weak for even the smallest model (fragmentation backstop done by the store, not by copy).
2. APP FIRST-RUN — where the real honesty lives. Detect RAM/chip, pick tier, say plainly which practices suit it: simpler practices (breath/body/emotions) complete on-device; exploration/parts work "a larger model helps — connect your own key or use hosted." Frame on-device as "private, runs entirely on your phone," NEVER "lite/basic" (a2j crippleware guardrail). Honesty is in WHICH practices each tier is pointed at, not in calling the small model bad.
3. WEBSITE — allowed to be a bit more concrete: one "Will it run on my phone?" section with honest RAM bands + reassurance that the app detects automatically. Still open with practice/values, not a spec table.

Android: read total RAM (ActivityManager.MemoryInfo), bucket into same bands as iOS, load matching model. Same capability vocabulary on both platforms, derived from RAM, never device name.

Vocabulary: give users EXPERIENCE names, never parameter counts (never "1B vs 4B"). E.g. "On-device (private)" -> "Your own key" -> "Hosted (deepest)", with a quiet non-anxious note on the on-device tier about which practices suit it. Pairs with the cost-transparency meter (meditation-pal-14s): radical honesty about both compute spend AND device capability = one coherent trust-building brand.

Correction captured here too: Apple Intelligence Foundation Models require 8GB RAM (iPhone 15 Pro+/16+), so they do NOT reach the iPhone 13 / 4GB floor — must ship our own model (MLC/llama.cpp/Core ML) for that tier. See meditation-pal-dbd (assumption needs fixing) and meditation-pal-0vb (validate the right model per RAM tier).

Touches: meditation-pal-toz (web page), meditation-pal-7rh (store submission), meditation-pal-a2j (honesty/values copy guardrail), meditation-pal-14s (cost meter), meditation-pal-dbd / meditation-pal-0vb (on-device tiering).
notes: 2026-05-27: on-device STT/TTS specifics for the tier picker (from shell-decision review, full detail on meditation-pal-nn1). STT tiers by RAM band: tiny.en (~75MB) -> base.en (~142MB) -> small.en (~180MB quantized, best quality, flagship). TTS: bundle Piper 'mid' voices (~20-60MB each) on all tiers. Models are DOWNLOAD-ON-DEMAND at first run (iOS On-Demand Resources / Android Play Asset Delivery or own CDN), NOT bundled in the store binary — keeps store download small; on-disk footprint ~100-500MB depending on tier, normal for the category. The first-run detect-and-recommend flow is the download decision point: detect RAM/chip -> recommend tier -> download matching model -> ALWAYS let the user opt out to hosted (never force the storage cost). Reinforces the existing experience-name vocabulary: 'On-device (private)' is the recommended default on capable devices, with a quiet note on which practices suit it — never 'lite/basic'.

title: Pre-launch: App Store AGPL license-grant exception
description:
Before submitting to the iOS App Store, publish a copyright-holder exception that grants Apple the right to distribute the binary under their standard terms.

**Why this is needed**: Apple's App Store Usage Rules (device-count limits, FairPlay DRM, no redistribution) impose restrictions that AGPL §6 forbids ('no further restrictions'). VLC famously got pulled from iOS in 2011 over this exact conflict. The FSF's position is that pure AGPL software cannot legally be distributed via the App Store unless the copyright holder grants an explicit additional permission.

**The fix**: as the copyright holder, add a `LICENSE-EXCEPTION.md` (or equivalent section in README/LICENSE) granting Apple a non-exclusive license to distribute under their terms — AGPL still applies to the source.

Example wording (adjust with legal review):
> The copyright holder additionally grants Apple, Inc. and any party operating under its terms a non-exclusive permission to distribute, link, and combine this software with Apple proprietary frameworks under Apple's standard App Store distribution terms, notwithstanding the restrictions of AGPL §6.

**Contributor caveat**: this only works cleanly if the user is sole copyright holder. Once external contributors gain copyright, they'd need to agree too — easiest pre-launch fix is to add a CONTRIBUTING.md note that submitting code is acknowledgment of the App Store exception, before accepting non-trivial PRs.

Google Play has no equivalent restriction, so this is iOS-only.

Spawned from 2026-05-09 license review. Should land before any App Store submission (related: meditation-pal-7rh).

title: Credits + metered billing backend (Stripe) [spec-first]
description: SPEC FIRST, then implement. Design the credits engine for the web demo, then build it. Core decision: price each session by METERED actual cost (LLM tokens + STT minutes + TTS chars) x margin multiplier (e.g. 1.5-2x), debited from a credit balance with a small pre-auth hold at session start. Metered debit is the direct fix for the cost-math trap meditation-pal-a2j flags (a daily power user's heavy use can never run at a loss). Stripe for payments (~3%, vs mobile's 30% store tax). Cover: credit-pack price points, the metered formula + margin, Stripe integration, sales-tax/VAT (Stripe Tax), chargebacks, and ToS for reselling LLM/voice access. Extends meditation-pal-a2j. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.
notes: MONEY MODEL DECISION (with dev): margin lives at PURCHASE, not in the debit (Model B). Credits debit at raw provider COST (1 credit = USD_PER_CREDIT = $0.05 of compute, tunable); PACK_MARKUP (2.5x) is applied only when selling credit packs. So per-session credit counts map 1:1 to real compute cost (transparent, easy to verify), and the markup is visible at checkout. Sales tax/VAT added on top via Stripe Tax (not from margin). assertSolvent(packs) now runs at boot and refuses to start if any pack's effective markup < 1/(1-worstCommission) (~1.22x EU). Packs: starter 50cr/$6, plus 110cr/$12, pro 240cr/$24 (~2.0-2.4x markup, volume discount on bigger). NOTE: do NOT also mark up the debit — that double-counts margin (the bug we fixed; packs were stale from the $0.01-credit era). CONTEXT STRATEGY: always FULL context (never rolling) — long-arc continuity matters for meditation; instead show a 'long conversations use credits faster' note. Removed the rolling-window cost lever from the plan.

title: Settings page overflows at larger text-scale settings
description:
The settings page has several px-based responsive breakpoints (e.g. 475px for the Save Settings wrap, 450px for the footer secondary buttons, 530px for various stack-into-column rules). When the user increases the in-app text scale, the actual content widens but the breakpoints don't shift, so layouts overflow before the next breakpoint fires.

Likely fix: convert the settings-page breakpoints from px to em so they scale with the root font-size (and the in-app text-scale setting, if it's wired to rem/root size). Container queries are a heavier alternative for components whose own width matters more than the viewport.

Low priority — only noticeable at significantly bumped text sizes.

title: Voice staleness: Settings default voice not applied to sessions
description: Changing the voice in the Settings page (settings.defaultVoice) doesn't affect the voice used in exploration OR the noting opener — a stale voice plays. ROOT CAUSE: loadSetup() (ui/src/settings.ts:183) merges defaultSetup < loadAppDefaults() (voice:settings.defaultVoice) < persisted 'preview:setup'. The persisted setup includes 'voice' and WINS, and persist() saves the whole setup (incl. voice) on any setup interaction. So once preview:setup has a voice, settings.defaultVoice never propagates. Both session.ts (buildTts(setup.voice), rebuildTts) and noting-session.ts (speakVia(setup.voice) opener; per-participant p.voice ?? setup.voice) read setup.voice. FIX DIRECTION (decide precedence): either propagate a Settings voice change into the persisted setup, or have loadSetup always inherit voice/rate/provider from app defaults unless a per-session override was explicitly set (track an 'explicitly overridden' flag), or unify on one voice source. Confirm which dropdown is canonical (Settings = global default vs Setup = per-session).
close_reason: Unified voice/rate onto app-settings as the single source of truth (option 3). loadSetup now always takes voice+ttsRate from app settings, clobbering any stale value persisted in preview:setup; the setup voice picker + speed slider + auto-select now write THROUGH to app settings (persistDefaultVoice) instead of preview:setup. Settings is canonical for voice/rate; provider/model stay per-session overridable. Fixes exploration voice AND noting opener (both read setup.voice). Regression test: tests/setup-voice-inheritance.test.ts.

title: Tauri build/release workflow — replace PyInstaller jobs for mac/win/linux
description:
Wire the Tauri shell into release so cutting a release ships the Rust/TS
app instead of the Python/PyInstaller frozen builds. Today release.sh →
build.yml runs PyInstaller for all three platforms; npm run tauri:build
works locally but isn't on any release path.

## Scope

1. **CI workflow** — either replace the macOS/Windows/Linux jobs in
   .github/workflows/build.yml or add a sibling tauri-release.yml.
   Each job runs `npm run tauri:build` after `npm ci` and the Tauri
   prereqs (Rust toolchain, cmake, C compiler for whisper-rs/espeak-rs).
   Upload the produced artifacts to the GH release like the current
   PyInstaller jobs do.

2. **macOS**: adapt the existing cert-import + notarytool keychain
   flow in build.yml (it already imports MACOS_CERTIFICATE +
   MACOS_CERTIFICATE_PWD, stores `notary` credentials). Tauri's bundler
   supports signing + notarization via env vars (APPLE_CERTIFICATE,
   APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_ID, APPLE_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID).
   Produces aloud-X.Y.Z-macOS.dmg.

3. **Windows**: Tauri produces MSI + NSIS installers. Decide which (or
   both). Code signing: the Python build is currently unsigned on
   Windows; if we want to sign, plumb a Windows cert via secrets.

4. **Linux**: Tauri produces .deb and AppImage. Match the current
   convention (AppImage) and the file naming
   (aloud-X.Y.Z-Linux.AppImage).

5. **release.sh**: today it reads `__version__` from src/__init__.py
   and lints Python with ruff. Move the version source to
   ts/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json (or ts/package.json) and lint TS+Rust
   instead (npm run typecheck + cargo check, plus cargo deny check).
   Keep the same UX (`scripts/release.sh patch|minor|major|X.Y.Z`).

6. **scripts/build-dmg.sh** — retire once the macOS GH job uses the
   Tauri bundler directly. Until then keep it as a fallback.

## Decision points (call these out in the PR)

- Replace PyInstaller jobs outright vs ship both for one cycle. Recommend
  replace outright once meditation-pal-sk8 lands (Python deletion); until
  then we could run both in parallel to validate.
- Bundler choice on Windows (MSI vs NSIS vs both).
- Whether to add Linux .deb alongside AppImage.

## Acceptance

- A fresh release (test branch or `redo`) produces three signed,
  notarized-where-applicable artifacts:
  - aloud-X.Y.Z-macOS.dmg (signed + notarized)
  - aloud-X.Y.Z-Windows.exe (or .msi)
  - aloud-X.Y.Z-Linux.AppImage
- Each artifact launches and the embedded Rust backend serves /api/*
  (verified via dev-docs/desktop.md's endpoint list).
- The PyInstaller path can be removed without losing release coverage.

## References

- Existing PyInstaller flow: .github/workflows/build.yml, scripts/build-dmg.sh
- Tauri shell: ts/src-tauri/, ts/package.json (tauri:build), dev-docs/desktop.md
- Build prereqs (cmake + C compiler + ort onnxruntime download): see
  dev-docs/desktop.md "Build prerequisites"
- Migration position: meditation-pal-sk8 (Python deletion) should land
  after this so release.sh can be flipped to Tauri only.
notes: Implemented on ts-core (2026-05-28, Opus 4.8), commit fa92709. Added .github/workflows/tauri-release.yml (mac/win/linux, parallel to PyInstaller per the run-both decision; artifacts -tauri suffixed to avoid collision). release.sh syncs version into tauri.conf.json + ts/package.json and lints TS/Rust. UNTESTED end-to-end — needs a real release run. Before first run: set repo var ALOUD_SERVER_URL (hosted origin baked into desktop UI) and confirm the Apple* secrets map. Cutover (sk8): delete build.yml, drop -tauri suffix, make tauri.conf.json the version source, strip ruff/PyInstaller from release.sh.

title: Lower pywebview min_size to modern-iPhone dimensions
description: Once mobile responsive layout work is done, lower the pywebview min_size from (1024, 600) to something corresponding to a modern iPhone — proposed (390, 700) so the app works at iPhone-13-class dimensions but isn't silly small. Verify the native window still works on macOS/Windows/Linux at the new minimum. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Mobile pricing & monetization spec
description:
Lock in the pricing structure for the standalone mobile app. Goals: accessible price, sustainable economics, no values compromise (no 'pay monthly to feel calm' framing).

Proposed structure (from 2026-05-09 design conversation):
- **App purchase**: ~$7.99 one-time ($5 instinct adjusted for inflation; below $9.99 'real app' threshold but above 'trinket' $4.99)
- **On-device LLM**: free, full functionality, indefinite — the real default
- **BYOK**: free tier, user's own Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter key
- **Hosted cloud**: optional upgrade
  - Pack: ~$4.99 / ~100 sessions (one-time, no recurring)
  - Subscription: ~$3.99/month unlimited (framed as 'we host the model for you', not 'meditation premium')
- **Welcome credit**: app purchase includes a small batch (e.g. 10) of cloud sessions to try the high-quality tier — onboarding hook without subscription pressure

Open math problem:
- Sonnet at ~$0.05–0.20 per 30-min session can underwater the $3.99/mo tier for daily users (30 sessions × $0.20 = $6 cost > $3.99 revenue). Solutions: cheaper default model on the hosted tier (Haiku / Sonnet small / open-weights on a cheap inference provider), soft monthly cap, or reprice.
- Apple/Google take 30% (15% for small business). Net economics need to work after that cut.

Deliverable: a spreadsheet covering median + heavy-user economics, final price points, and an honest copy spec for how the tiers are presented in-app and on the marketing page.

Related: dbd (cloud LLM architecture), toz (web page that may communicate pricing).

Cost lever (added 2026-05-25 by Claude): prompt caching materially changes the per-session math above. The system prompt is currently cached at the default 5-min ephemeral TTL, but sessions have long silent gaps (the 300s check-in is a canned phrase, not an LLM call), so the cache frequently expires between turns and each call repays a ~1.25x cache write instead of a ~0.1x read. A 1h (or adaptive) TTL keeps the prefix warm across pauses and lowers hosted-tier cost. See meditation-pal-ttl for analysis and options. Note: only the direct Anthropic API path can set TTL — the claude-proxy/CLI subscription path cannot.

title: Deploy: ui/dist static host + standalone proxy on Fly/Render (HTTPS)
description: Infra for the web demo. Frontend: ui/dist (Vite build) is static — host on the existing static host (e.g. GitHub Pages) or any CDN. Backend: the standalone proxy on a small always-on VM (Fly ~$5-10/mo, Render Starter $7, or a cheap VPS); NOT AWS-required and cheaper/simpler than AWS at trial scale. Real TLS (mic needs a secure context; cert.py self-signed is LAN-only). Single process is fine at trial scale; note Redis/sticky-sessions only matter if we later make the backend stateful. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: API key management & config (mobile)
description: Secure storage for API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, ElevenLabs) using expo-secure-store. Settings screen for: choosing LLM provider and model, entering API keys, selecting TTS engine and voice, default speaking rate, default verbosity. Persist all config with AsyncStorage. First-run onboarding flow to set up at least one LLM provider.

title: Cloud-STT endpoint for web (Groq Whisper); decouple server-whisper-stt from Flask
description:
ts/ui/src/adapters/server-whisper-stt.ts currently posts PCM to Flask's /api/stt/whisper. For a hosted demo that can't depend on someone's local Flask+Whisper. Add a cloud-STT path: forward captured audio to Groq Whisper Large v3 Turbo (~$0.04/hr audio) via the standalone backend, and repoint the adapter/endpoint accordingly.

Note (cost): meditation is mostly silence (VAD-gated), so billed audio is small — well under a cent per session even with overhead. Relates to meditation-pal-0ao (native STT validation) which already notes cloud-Whisper fallback as the documented escape hatch. Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.
close_reason: Implemented: POST /v1/stt forwards raw PCM to Groq whisper-large-v3-turbo (providers/stt.ts encodeWav + transcribeWithGroq; routes/stt.ts), metered fractionally by audio duration (priceSttSeconds). UI: ServerWhisperSttEngine gained authProvider; stt-picker.createServerAloudStt; session.ts routes STT through the server on the hosted provider. Needs GROQ_API_KEY. Server 62 tests green.

title: Mobile responsive: settings page
description: Make /settings usable on phones. Stack form-row form-group-half pairs, mobile-friendly section spacing, ensure all the provider/TTS controls work at 390px. Mostly mechanical. Part of meditation-pal-leo.

title: Adaptive local context management (latency-triggered, invisible) — preserve long-arc continuity
description:
FUTURE / THEORETICAL — do not pull unless latency is actually observed. Split out from meditation-pal-77f so the P1 caching win stays clean.

Worry: with FULL context, a long session re-sends the entire transcript each turn, so a local model (e.g. Gemma 4 26B on a MacBook) re-processes a growing prompt and response latency could climb as the session deepens. NOTE (2026-05-24): the dev reports no meaningful latency on local sessions so far — this is precautionary, not a live problem.

Idea (the dev's framing): build it in as an INVISIBLE, local-only adaptive check — measure per-turn latency, and only if it crosses a threshold, start managing context (e.g. begin summarizing the oldest turns into a running synopsis) so responses stay snappy. Stay dormant/no-op otherwise.

HARD CONSTRAINT: long-arc continuity is load-bearing for exploration quality — a good session calls back to material from 30+ min earlier ('the balance from earlier', 'little Alex'). Any trimming MUST preserve that (running summary of trimmed turns, not a naive rolling window). This is a quality-sensitive design task, unlike the caching ticket which is quality-neutral.

Applies to all paths but motivated by local. Companion to meditation-pal-77f.

title: Web Flask removal: serve /api/* non-inference endpoints from ts/server (Hono)
description:
For the web target, replace Flask's non-inference /api endpoints with Hono routes in ts/server so the web build needs no Python. Web users get STT/TTS via cloud forwarding (already in ts/server) or browser-native adapters, so NO local Whisper/Piper is needed here.

Port to Hono: /api/providers, /api/models/<provider>, /api/voices (static catalog), /api/system-info, /api/tts-engines. claude_proxy and the /api/open-* shell escapes are desktop-only (omit on web). Also serve the built ui/dist statically.

This + the desktop bead together let Flask be deleted (final step of dev-docs/ts-migration-status.md order-of-operations).
notes: Implemented on ts-core (2026-05-28, Opus 4.8), commit 89fd698. Hono now serves /app/v1/{system-info,providers,models/:provider,voices} (routes/app.ts + providers/models.ts) + optional static ui/dist via ALOUD_UI_DIR. system-info carries desktop:false and is-desktop.ts now keys off that field (was: mere reachability) so the web build keeps desktop features off. Server tests 73 (incl app-backend.test.ts). Depends on the a8677e7 route rename. NEEDS VERIFICATION: run ui:build with VITE_ALOUD_SERVER_URL set + the server, confirm the web build reaches /app/v1 + /cloud/v1 cross-origin and that BYOK model lists work when a key is entered.

title: Port audio.js VAD niceties (speculative / adaptive silence / pre-buffer) to TS
description: ts/ui/src/adapters/server-whisper-stt.ts has an intentionally simple RMS+silence-timeout VAD; its header says the Flask UI's audio.js niceties (speculative submission, adaptive silence thresholding, pre-buffer for utterance onset) should be ported 'when the loop is solid'. Port them so the web demo's turn-taking matches the Python pipeline's snappiness. Reference: src/web/static/js/audio.js (submitSpeculative/finalizeSpeculative, adaptive silenceNeeded ramp, preBuffer). Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.
notes: Pre-buffer for utterance onset landed on ts-core (2026-05-27): server-whisper-stt.ts now keeps the mic stream + AudioContext alive across turns (kills the per-turn getUserMedia latency that ate a barge-in's first second) and prepends a ~250ms onset pre-buffer ring so the first word isn't clipped. With this, all three audio.js niceties (speculative / adaptive silence / pre-buffer) are ported. Remaining before close: hands-on barge-in verification in the Tauri desktop window (user testing).

title: Web demo of aloud. (hosted, accounts + credits)
description:
Umbrella for making aloud. accessible as a hosted web demo (distinct from the marketing page in meditation-pal-toz). A stranger hits a URL, signs in, gets 1-2 free sessions on a cheap/on-device-class model, then buys credits to access premium LLMs and voices, with margin built in.

Key architectural reframe (2026-05-23 design conversation): the web demo is a DEPLOYMENT TARGET of the TS rewrite (meditation-pal-2qg), not a separate build. The ts/ui Vite build (ui/dist) is static and can sit on the existing static host (e.g. GitHub Pages); only a backend proxy needs a server. The web STT/TTS/LLM adapters already exist under ts/ui/src/adapters (web-speech-stt, server-whisper-stt, server-tts, claude-proxy-http) but currently point at the desktop Flask backend — the web demo must decouple them onto a standalone hosted backend.

Relationship to mobile plans: this REUSES meditation-pal-dbd (cloud LLM proxy) and meditation-pal-a2j (pricing/monetization). The web variant differs from mobile in three ways worth a fresh spec: (1) no 30% app-store tax — Stripe ~3%, roughly doubling margin headroom and softening a2j's cost-math trap; (2) auth is web OAuth (Google/Apple/etc.) not IAP; (3) abuse surface is wide open (no app-store gating), so free-tier quota/abuse controls are load-bearing.

Blocked by the TS rewrite reaching browser-usable parity (meditation-pal-2qg).
notes: ARCH DECISION (2026-05-24, with developer): the server lives in the SAME public AGPL repo as a workspace package (ts/server, @aloud/server), NOT a separate/private repo. Rationale: developer wants the server open source too (privacy + ownership values; meditation app). Once open, AGPL flips from liability to asset — its network-use clause guarantees the deployed server stays open. The only arguably-sensitive value, the margin multiplier, is intentionally PUBLISHED (GET /v1/me/models). Non-disruptive workspace layout chosen to run in parallel with the TS conversion: ts/src and ts/ui were NOT moved; ts/package.json gained a 'workspaces' field; the full packages/{core,ui,server} rename is deferred to a coordinated step later. Scaffold landed on branch ts-server (see ts/server/README.md). Next session: do NOT default to a private repo or to scaffolding from scratch.

title: Hosted pass-through abuse & cost controls (web-first)
description:
Cost-control + abuse-mitigation for the free hosted pass-through (LLM/TTS/STT) so account-farming or a viral/scripted spike can't produce an unbounded bill. Web-first: the strong mobile primitives (App Store receipt, App Attest, Play Integrity) don't exist in the browser — on web you only have leaky identifiers (IP, cookie, OAuth, CAPTCHA), so identity can't be the safety boundary. Build the defense around bounded pools + money instead.

Business-model context (2026-05-27): app, website, and desktop program are ALL free. Revenue is donations + an optional paid pass-through for people who want us to conveniently host LLM/TTS/STT. New accounts get a fixed one-time batch of free credits (all they ever get free); users can buy more. Average cost/session is shown up front so a user knows before starting whether they need to top up.

Two distinct abuse vectors:
1. Volume / farming — one person making new accounts to reset the free credit grant.
2. Repurposing — scripting our endpoints as a free general-purpose LLM/TTS/STT API. This is the vector most likely to actually cost real money, and it's independent of identity.

Controls, in priority order:
- **Systemwide daily budget cap (hard backstop).** A ceiling on total free spend/credits per day across all users, per service. Bounds the worst case absolutely regardless of how many accounts are farmed — this is what makes imperfect Sybil resistance acceptable. UX: check at session START; if the daily free pool is exhausted, don't start NEW free sessions, but let any in-flight session finish (never cut someone off mid-meditation). Implies a start-time cost estimate / reservation.
- **Resume / continue-from-history counts as a session START.** Resuming a past session (the continue-from-history flow: history.ts sets sessionStorage 'continueFrom' → session.ts loadExchanges) must pass the same daily-cap + credit check and reserve estimated cost up front, exactly like a fresh session. The 'let in-flight sessions finish' carve-out applies ONLY to a session already actively running — NEVER to resuming a historical one, or it's a trivial bypass (sit over the cap, resume an old session, keep spending free). Resume replays the prior transcript as context, so its first request is often LARGER/costlier than a fresh start — estimate on the actual replayed payload, not a flat per-session figure.
- **Constrain the proxy server-side (kills vector 2).** Accept only the structured meditation request and assemble the real provider call server-side — fixed/whitelisted system prompt, capped max_tokens, the app's own message shape; never forward arbitrary request bodies. Same for TTS/STT (cap input length, no arbitrary params). Highest-value control, needs no identity. (See the open-relay note from the ts-core review: src/web/llm_proxy.py currently forwards bodies unchanged.)
- **Per-account free credit grant (a monetary balance, not a fixed session count).** One-time batch on signup; depletes with use; buy-more to continue. Users pick ANY model, with transparent per-model cost shown before a session begins — so the same grant might buy ~1 Opus session or ~10 Gemini Flash sessions. Start-time estimates, credit deduction, and daily-cap accounting are all denominated in money at the CHOSEN model's rate (a cheap default is fine, but it's a default, not a restriction).
- **Light per-client friction.** IP + cookie rate limits; a bot challenge (e.g. Cloudflare Turnstile) on web. On mobile, optionally use device attestation (App Attest / Play Integrity) for a cleaner per-device identity + a more generous cap — a bonus, not the web baseline.
- **BYOK + on-device as the always-free escape hatch.** Every client can run the user's own key or an on-device model, so hitting the free cap never blocks a real user — they switch and keep going. This is what lets us cap the free pool aggressively without harming legit users.
- **Cheap default model on the free hosted tier** (e.g. Haiku, not Sonnet) to lower the abuse ceiling and stretch the daily pool. (Ties to the cost-math TODO in dbd / a2j.)

Why NOT 'sign in with Google/Apple' as the limiter: Google accounts are free to mint, so federated sign-in barely raises farming cost while adding friction and excluding accountless / iPhone-on-web users. Identity is at most a secondary fairness tool (per-user daily share), never the boundary.

All of this lives on the hosted backend (see dbd); some pieces (attestation, receipt checks) require real-device testing. Decisions here are small and align with the existing pricing direction; the architecture supports staying fully free (cap + fallback) or layering an optional paid 'higher/unmetered cap' tier later without re-architecting.

title: API key UX: Get-key link + paste-from-clipboard for all providers
description: Add per-input action row: 'Get a key' opens provider console in new tab; 'Paste from clipboard' uses navigator.clipboard.readText (validates against key prefix). Covers anthropic/openai/groq/openrouter/venice + elevenlabs. Highlights paste button when user returns from console tab.

title: TS UI: revisit rewrites under lift-first rule
description:
Per the lift-first rule (saved 2026-05-14): default to 1:1 port of existing UI behavior, save new ideas as separate notes. Several pieces of the TS preview were written from scratch rather than lifted; redo them to match the existing app.

Pieces to revisit:
- Session orb — existing animations live in src/web/static/css/. Currently I shipped multi-state breathing/listening/thinking/speaking/holding modes; existing orb has its own behavior with kasina mode, drag-to-position, rainbow easter egg, etc.
- Theme toggle — port chrome.js theme block more directly (icon SVGs, 8-clicks-in-4s easter egg)
- Voice picker — existing voice-picker.js + _voice_modal.html has modal UI with previews, speed slider, voice categorization, 'needs download' state. Current TS preview has a basic dropdown.
- General CSS — large parts of src/web/static/css/ haven't been lifted; my own CSS is fine for layout but loses visual identity

After lifting, retain quality but stage refactors / new ideas in a separate backlog ticket so the user can decide which to keep.
close_reason:
Audited all four flagged pieces against the Flask UI; outcome:
- Theme toggle: ALREADY at parity — same sun/moon SVGs, the 8-clicks-in-4s 'the system… is down…' easter egg, same theme/themeMode persistence keys (ts/ui/src/theme.ts). TS even adds a compatible time-of-day fallback. No action.
- Voice picker: ALREADY at parity — full modal (not the 'basic dropdown' the ticket described), per-voice preview, speed slider, tier grouping, needs-download/uninstall states (ts/ui/src/voice-picker.ts). No action.
- General CSS: essentially fine — ts/ui/src/style.css @imports the entire Flask style.css, so tokens/fonts/animations/identity carry over. Only divergence is TS-only layout classes using magic numbers; moved that cleanup note to the backlog (meditation-pal-1au), not a parity blocker.
- Session orb / kasina mode: THE real gap, now fixed. The old TS stub set body[data-kasina] with no matching CSS rule (fully inert). Lifted initKasinaMode() 1:1 from src/web/static/js/session.js into ts/ui/src/views/session.ts: click-orb-to-enter, 600ms FLIP transition between nav and 140px center gaze object, DOM reposition to <body>, forced-dark theme override (restored on exit), mouse+touch drag-to-position, shake + 4-quick-click rainbow easter egg with 2s cooldown and ember recolor (setRainbow), click-outside-to-exit. Now drives the imported .orb-kasina/.kasina-active/.orb-rainbow CSS. SPA-correctness: document-level drag/click listeners scoped to an AbortController and torn down on endSession (the Flask MPA reloaded per nav, so never needed this). typecheck + ui build clean, 134 tests pass. NOTE: structural lift verified by typecheck/build only — visual smoke test of the FLIP/drag/shake interactions still recommended.

title: Port error-toast system (showErrorToast)
description: TS has no toast system; LLM/STT errors surface only as inline #voice-status text and out-of-session errors have no surface. Port old ui.js:showErrorToast + .error-toast (CSS already imported, unused) and wire into error paths. Audit Tier-1.
close_reason: Added reusable toast.ts (showErrorToast) — port of ui.js, app-wide so out-of-session errors have a surface; uses the already-imported .error-toast CSS, fades in/out, auto-dismisses 5s, click-to-dismiss. Wired into session LLM-error catch (toast + resume listening) and STT-error path (toasts once per error transition, no spam). typecheck + 176 tests green.

title: Desktop Flask removal: local backend for Tauri (/api/* replacement)
description:
Production Tauri builds have no Vite proxy, so the bundled UI's fetch('/api/...') has no backend. Replace each Flask /api endpoint with a desktop-local equivalent so a Flask-free .app works. Decision (meditation-pal-nn1): native Rust in Tauri for local inference.

Approach: mirror ui/src/server-base.ts with an apiUrl() helper pointed at an embedded local server (axum) or Tauri commands in the desktop build; dev keeps the Vite proxy.

Endpoints to replace: /api/stt/whisper (whisper-rs), /api/voices + /api/voices/preview (Piper ONNX; macOS 'say' fallback), /api/providers, /api/models, /api/tts-engines, /api/system-info, /api/llm/claude_proxy/complete (spawn claude CLI), /api/open-config-folder, /api/open-sessions-folder, /api/open-voice-settings (shell escapes).

Piper-via-ONNX is the one moderate-risk piece; if it becomes a tar pit, drop just Piper to a sidecar/binary (hybrid). whisper-rs is mature. See dev-docs/desktop.md.
notes: Deferred work landed on ts-core (2026-05-28, Opus 4.8). Commits: route rename a8677e7; anthropic BYOK proxy d9b031d; ollama restart/upgrade/install + settings controls 944abea; live model lists via key-forwarding + /cloud/v1 double-prefix bugfix 45a9591. All: cargo check + typecheck + tests green (UI 176). NEEDS MANUAL DESKTOP VERIFICATION (tauri:build runtime): the ollama restart 'detect+pkill+respawn+poll' dance, model-list fetches per provider, and the anthropic proxy round-trip — none exercised by unit tests. /app/v1/tts-engines still intentionally unimplemented (no UI consumer).

title: Cross-platform credits: sell on web (Stripe), spend on mobile — bypass IAP cut
description:
STRATEGY + SPEC. Make credits a portable account-bound currency so users can BUY them on the web store (Stripe, ~3-5% all-in) and SPEND them in the mobile app — instead of buying via Apple/Google IAP and losing 15-30%. This is the 'Pokemon Go / Fortnite web store' model and is potentially the single biggest lever on the economics meditation-pal-a2j worries about (a2j assumes a 30% store tax that this largely removes).

Why it's newly viable (verified 2026-05, but UNSETTLED — re-check before building):
- US: since the Apr/May-2025 Epic v. Apple contempt ruling, App Store apps may link out to external web purchases with NO Apple commission and no entitlement. Dec-2025 Ninth Circuit largely upheld this.
- RISK: the Ninth Circuit said Apple MAY set a 'reasonable commission' on externally-linked purchases; the framework is still being decided on remand and Apple is seeking Supreme Court review (rehearing denied Mar-2026, remand proceeding Apr-2026). So US ~0% commission is real today but could change.
- EU: NOT free — External Purchase Link entitlement required and Core Technology Commission (~12-20%) applies. Model EU separately.
- You take on payment processing, sales tax/VAT, fraud, and compliance for web-sold credits.

Design notes:
- Credits must be account-bound (requires the OAuth/account model, meditation-pal-rfb) and balance-checked server-side by the proxy so they're spendable cross-device.
- The mobile app links out to the web checkout (compliant external link), user returns and balance reflects the top-up.
- Keep an IAP path as fallback for users who won't leave the app / for jurisdictions where external links are restricted; accept the cut there.

Depends on the credits/billing engine (meditation-pal-8sj) and accounts (meditation-pal-rfb). Extends meditation-pal-a2j. Child of the web demo epic; serves mobile economics.

title: Barge-in: add echoCancellation + restore onset preservation (server-whisper)
description: Keep new barge-in.ts wrapper arch but (1) request echoCancellation:true on the parallel mic stream so TTS doesn't self-trip (old audio.js did), and (2) preserve the triggering audio so interrupting words aren't lost — feasible only on the server-whisper chunk path. Audit Tier-3.
notes: Onset hardening round 2 (c45ae9c) after live test showed first-word clip persisted: PRE_BUFFER_MS 1400->2000, and added an AudioContext statechange->resume keepalive so the continuous capture context can't get suspended between turns (which would stall the pre-buffer during TTS and lose the onset). Still needs live re-test to confirm. Separately: out-of-credits/auth errors now surface clearly (describeHostedError, commit 69239a0/79f030e) — the aloud provider routes LLM+STT+TTS through credits, which was the cause of the 'STT broke / no speech / out of credits' confusion in testing.

title: Narrow/mobile session polish: align noting with exploration
description: From mobile testing. FIXED this round: (1) noting now sets body[data-session-active] -> bottom-nav hidden in noting + End/History appear in the More sheet; (2) wireNav is session-aware -> data-nav links (bottom-nav/top History) route through the end-confirm overlay instead of silently tearing down (no-confirm-on-nav bug); (3) exploration mute status no longer sticks on 'Muted' after unmute; (4) .mobile-more-glyph uses the Knewave brand font. REMAINING (visual/design): noting bottom control bar doesn't match exploration (mute button wraps to its own line at narrow width; missing controls); and on mobile the thin top bar only drags by logo/orb (session content likely paints over the empty middle) — want the whole top strip draggable.
notes: IMPLEMENTED (commit 50c88fe): ported full exploration control bar to noting — session timer, TTS-toggle (gates speakVia; circle runs silently but turns still advance), embers level control (reuse embers.ts), kasina toggle. No Just Listen, no live voice picker (per-participant voices live in setup). Extracted kasina into shared ts/ui/src/kasina.ts (per dev decision: extract not duplicate); exploration calls it verbatim, behavior unchanged. Noting teardown now clears timer / unmounts embers / exits kasina / aborts listeners. Dropped dead .input-row-solo CSS. typecheck+tests(180)+ui:build all green. STILL NEEDS HANDS-ON: kasina FLIP feel in noting, bottom-bar layout at narrow width, TTS-toggle behavior, embers in noting — UI feel can't be verified headlessly. Prior top-strip-drag item already handled by body[data-session-active] .nav pointer-events rule (earlier commit).

title: Cloud LLM proxy for standalone mobile
description:
Server-side LLM access for the standalone mobile app, structured as a tiered offering rather than a single proxy.

Tiers (per 2026-05-09 design conversation):
- **Free / on-device**: small LLM running locally (Apple Intelligence Foundation Models on iOS 18+, Gemma/Phi/Llama 3.2 via MLC-LLM or llama.cpp on Android). Always available, no network, full privacy. Default mode.
- **BYOK**: user supplies their own Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter key. Free tier — power users who want best quality at their own cost.
- **Hosted cloud**: paid access to a curated high-quality model. Two purchase shapes:
  - Session pack: ~$4.99 for ~100 sessions (one-time, no recurring)
  - Subscription: ~$3.99/month unlimited (clearly framed as paying for compute, not for meditation content)

Open questions / TODOs:
- Cost math: Sonnet at ~$0.05–0.20 per 30-min session could underwater the $3.99/mo tier for heavy users. Either choose a cheaper default model (Haiku, Sonnet 4 small, etc.), put a soft cap, or reprice. Needs spreadsheet pass before commit.
- Hosted backend stack — keep Python Flask, or rewrite in TypeScript so the mobile + desktop stories converge? See separate decision ticket.
- Abuse / quota / auth model for hosted tier (per-user keys, sign-in with Apple/Google).
- Transparency copy: ensure the on-device tier is honestly framed as 'real and complete', not crippleware.

Phase 1 (phone-as-remote, jim) doesn't need any of this — it talks to the user's own desktop. This ticket is for Phase 2 (standalone install).

Cost lever (added 2026-05-25 by Claude): prompt-cache TTL is a direct knob on the hosted-tier cost math above. The default 5-min ephemeral cache on the system prompt dies during meditation's long silent gaps, so calls repay cache writes (~1.25x) rather than reads (~0.1x). A 1h or adaptive TTL keeps the prefix warm. See meditation-pal-ttl. Caveats: TTL control exists only on the direct Anthropic API provider, not the claude-proxy/CLI path; and today only the system prompt is cached (not the growing conversation), so confirm the rendered prompt clears the model's min-cacheable size (~2048 tokens Sonnet 4.6 / 4096 Opus) before relying on it.

title: Implement text-to-speech (mobile)
description: Implement TTS for meditation responses. Options: (1) expo-speech - wraps platform TTS (AVSpeechSynthesizer on iOS, Android TTS) - free, offline, decent quality. (2) ElevenLabs API - highest quality, costs money, requires network. Start with expo-speech (platform voices). Must handle: configurable speaking rate (default 110 WPM), voice selection, interruptibility (stop speaking when user starts talking), queuing of responses.

title: Audio session & background handling (mobile)
description: Handle the trickiest mobile-specific challenge: audio sessions. iOS requires proper AVAudioSession category configuration (playAndRecord for simultaneous mic+speaker). Android needs foreground service for background audio. Must handle: phone calls interrupting session, app backgrounding (keep listening/speaking), notification sounds, Bluetooth headphones connecting/disconnecting, Do Not Disturb mode. This is the make-or-break reliability task.

title: Voice uninstall button doesn't work (desktop)
description: Settings voice picker Uninstall reportedly does nothing. Flow looks correct end-to-end: settings.ts uninstallVoice -> uninstallVoiceModel POST appUrl('/tts/uninstall-model') -> server.rs tts_uninstall_model -> tts.rs uninstall_model (find_piper by name, remove .onnx/.onnx.json from piper_dir), then refreshVoiceList. Download uses the identical name/engine path and works, so find_piper resolution is likely fine. Most likely causes: (a) BUNDLED piper voices live outside piper_dir, so uninstall_model finds no file -> returns 'not_found' with 200 OK -> frontend reports success but the voice remains; or (b) engine not 'piper' on the row -> backend 400 -> 'Could not uninstall' alert. DIAGNOSTIC NEEDED: does clicking pop an error alert, or silently report success while the voice stays? Couldn't reproduce headless.
close_reason: Root cause: window.confirm() is a silent no-op in the Tauri webview, so uninstallVoice aborted at 'if (!confirm(...)) return;' before any fetch — no dialog, nothing happened (Download has no confirm, so it worked). Added promise-based in-app dialog.ts (confirmDialog/alertDialog) + themed .app-dialog CSS, and replaced every window.confirm()/alert() in the UI (voice uninstall + download-error, history Delete, Ollama remove-model + error). Fixes the same latent bug in history-delete and ollama-remove too. typecheck + 176 tests green. NEEDS runtime confirmation on next desktop launch.

title: Privacy/data-handling spec: retention toggle (no-retention default only for multi-user hosted)
description:
Spec how session data (intimate meditation transcripts) is retained, with privacy copy and per-provider disclosure.

**Key correction (2026-05-23): retention is a TOGGLE, and the default DEPENDS on deployment context — do NOT make ephemeral the global default.**
- **Local / single-user (desktop, on-device mobile):** keep session history by DEFAULT — this is the current behavior (sessions auto-save; there's a History view). It's the user's own device; retention is a feature they rely on. Per-session opt-out already exists conceptually as Private Session Mode (meditation-pal-ht8).
- **Multi-user hosted (the web demo / cloud backend):** default to NO server-side retention of transcripts (ephemeral). This is where storing strangers' intimate disclosures is a real risk. Still a toggle — a signed-in user could opt IN to saving their own history server-side if we offer accounts — but off by default.

So: one retention setting, context-aware default (local on / hosted off), surfaced clearly to the user.

**Also in scope:**
- Short, clear privacy statement.
- Per-provider data-flow disclosure: where audio/text actually goes (e.g. Web Speech API ships audio to Google; Groq/Anthropic API retention terms; on-device = nothing leaves the device).
- Tie the "nothing is stored" promise to the privacy-as-positioning angle (see related positioning work) — for hosted, prove it in the UI.

Relates to meditation-pal-ht8 (private session mode = the per-session toggle). Blocked by meditation-pal-2qg.

title: Onboarding wizard for new users
description:
Guided first-run flow: LLM provider setup, voice selection with Piper recommendation, quality prompt. Currently a single settings page with a welcome header — could become a step-by-step wizard.

Mobile note (added 2026-05-09 by Claude): now that the form is mobile-shaped (responsive layout closed), a wizard implementation could lean into a 1-screen-per-decision flow on phone — intention → focus → vibe → directiveness → start, each occupying its own screen with the bottom nav reduced or replaced by Next/Back. Would feel native on phone and is straightforward to also render on desktop as either the same screens or an overview.

Mobile LLM tier picker (added 2026-05-09 by Claude): on the standalone mobile app, the onboarding LLM choice will be a 3-way pick rather than a provider list:
1. **On-device** (default, free, private)
2. **Use your own API key** (free, advanced)
3. **Cloud upgrade** (metered credits — see meditation-pal-8sj)

The wizard should make on-device feel like a complete, real choice — not a trial. The cloud upgrade should be reachable in one tap but not pushed.

title: Phase 1A: Structured Logging
Port mobile bottom-nav + More sheet (also fixes footer float-up)

Porting the real bottom-nav makes the legacy rule correct and fixes both. Audit Tier-1/Tier-2.

title: Structured TS↔Flask parity audit (walk every feature, log all divergences)
description:
Stop discovering TS-rewrite parity gaps one manual reload at a time. Do a deliberate, feature-by-feature pass comparing the Flask app (src/web) against the TS app (ts/ui), logging EVERY divergence in one go, then fix as a finite checklist.

Why: most session-view bugs this cycle (double user bubble, missing opener, dead typing indicator, mic-state icon, in-session voice rebuild, glooow preview phrase, bottom-bar layout, kasina orb) were lift-first violations — pieces written from scratch in the TS preview instead of ported. Each surfaced only through hands-on testing. Strongly implies more undiscovered gaps.

Scope: walk each screen/flow — setup, session (idle/listening/thinking/speaking/holding/kasina/rainbow), noting, history, settings (every pane), voice picker, about, onboarding/tour, error toasts, mobile bottom nav — and for each Flask feature note PRESENT / MISSING / DIFFERENT in TS with file:symbol. Output a checklist; fix or file sub-tickets.

Relates to meditation-pal-cfy (closed; orb lift) and meditation-pal-1au (TS-idea backlog).
notes: Audit done 2026-05-28 (static code-reading pass, 5 parallel readers) → dev-docs/ts-parity-audit.md. Found ~30 divergences across 5 severity tiers. Tier-1 functional regressions: noting Add-participant button hard-disabled (multi-participant circles unreachable); mobile bottom-nav/More-sheet entirely missing (End/History/Settings unreachable mid-session on phones); no non-speech-marker filter on user turns; Begin never disabled for unavailable provider; provider auto-select/order lost; error toasts gone; voice commands (mute/hold) unimplemented though tooltips advertise them; no-voices warning gone. Tier-2 (dev-flagged bugs, all confirmed): window-mode + network dropdowns are intentional disabled stubs (no backend); update-check button is a disabled stub with no endpoint on either TS target; settings/setup footer floats up at <=767px because imported legacy CSS lifts it 3.75rem to clear a .bottom-nav the TS UI never renders. NOT run in-product — pair with human hands-on pass. Tier-1/2 items are candidate sub-tickets.
close_reason: Audit complete → dev-docs/ts-parity-audit.md (5-reader static pass, ~30 divergences in 5 tiers). Fixes filed as sub-tickets 0tb/fsg/8zj/d35/3ru/1s6/cli/5fc (+deferred 438/xwp), all wired as sk8 blockers.

description: Multiple simultaneous users will bottleneck on Whisper STT. Options: request queue, smaller model option, or OpenAI Whisper API fallback.

title: Private session mode (no history)
description:
Add a 'private session' toggle on the index page that, when checked, prevents the session from being written to history. Useful for sensitive sessions (grief, trauma, anything the user wants to leave no trace of).

Design notes (from end-of-day-2026-05-09 brainstorm with Claude):

- UI: a checkbox or toggle near the Begin Session button. Unchecked by default. Persistence preference (remember last value) is debatable — defaulting to off each session is safer than accidentally leaving 'private' on.
- Behavior: skip the writes that build the session JSON file at session end. The doEndSession flow already has a 'showSkipSave' branch for the End-Without-Saving button — private mode is essentially 'always skip save', set up-front instead of decided at the end.
- Conversation transcript should still display in the live UI; only the persistence is suppressed.

Related ergonomic improvement to bundle (suggested by Claude):

- For NON-private sessions, add periodic auto-saves (e.g. every 30s or after every assistant turn) so a user who sits with a long session doesn't lose it to a crash, browser close, or accidental reload. This is independent of private mode but spec'd here because private mode highlights the persistence story.
notes: Auto-save cost constraint (added 2026-05-25): periodic checkpoints (every ~30s or per turn) must NOT run the LLM summarizer — only persist the raw session JSON (exchanges + usage + metadata). The summary is an extra LLM completion (generateSessionSummary), which would be wasteful/expensive to run on every checkpoint, especially on pricier hosted models. Generate the summary ONCE at session end (current behavior), and have checkpoints save with an empty/placeholder summary (or reuse the last one). i.e. decouple 'persist state' from 'summarize'.

title: Implement speech-to-text (mobile)

Options to evaluate: (1) expo-speech-recognition or react-native-voice for platform-native STT (Apple Speech / Android SpeechRecognizer) - simplest, no model downloads, good accuracy. (2) whisper.cpp via react-native bridge for on-device Whisper - more control, works offline, but larger app size (~40-150MB for model). Recommendation: start with platform-native STT, add Whisper option later. Must handle: continuous listening, VAD/silence detection for turn boundaries, background audio interruptions, microphone permissions.

title: Mass-account / abuse detection for free-tier signups (velocity + circuit breaker)
